extraction.one_line_summary: VAN DER POEL Mathieu won by a quarter bike length over VAN AERT Wout after both survived ALAPHILIPPE's race-splitting attack before the Paterberg
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE attacked on climbs before final Paterberg specifically to shed VAN AERT/VAN DER POEL, cracking peloton but crashed on motorbike.
VAN AERT mysteriously dropped through peloton mid-race but recovered explosively, suggesting mechanical or tactical repositioning rather than weakness.
VAN DER POEL deliberately waited beyond 300m to go despite time advantage, judging sprint distance psychology against VAN AERT perfectly.
BETTIOL and major favorites caught out by ALAPHILIPPE's timing on earlier hellingen, revealing World Champion's attack placement fragmented even prepared teams.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: had race-winning form and attacked strategically to isolate VAN AERT/VAN DER POEL but crashed into motorbike before finale
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: recovered from mid-race positional crisis to contest sprint, showing resilience but quarter-wheel margin suggests VAN DER POEL has edge
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: controlled finale wait to 200m despite time advantage shows sophisticated sprint timing against world-class opponent
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: negative: completely wrong-footed by ALAPHILIPPE's attack sequence despite being defending champion
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: neutral: salvaged third from distance but never in contention when race split on climbs
